item.description:
ChainLink 0.3.498

CORE body temperature, smarter training plans, and a stack of fixes.

New: CORE body temperature (greenteg CORE sensor)

  • Ride with a CORE sensor paired to your head unit and ChainLink reads the data straight from your ride files — nothing to set up. You get a Core Temp graph on the ride page and a plain-English heat dose ("32 min at or above 38.5 °C — a solid heat dose").
  • The AI coach sees your measured heat exposure alongside HRV, and travel advice for hot events is graded against your actual heat sessions from the last three weeks instead of a guess.
  • It's not just new rides: on first launch this build quietly re-reads your existing ride files, so past rides recorded with a CORE sensor get their temperature graphs and heat doses too — no re-import needed.

Training plans got a bigger playbook

  • Generated plans now vary the structure of workouts, not just the length: over-unders, ladders, 40/20 microbursts, hard-start reps, float sets, cadence drills and more. No shape repeats until you've seen them all, and the session card always describes exactly what the exported workout will make you ride.
  • One active plan at a time: starting an event plan now ends a running maintenance block (with a note) instead of double-counting your rides.
  • Maintenance plans gained the full action menu — adjust the block, share it as a PDF, end it.

Garmin Connect: history import hardened

  • The full-history import now paces itself around Garmin's rate limits, takes a breather and carries on when they bite, and re-checks the whole account once so previously missed rides are picked up automatically.
  • A second device no longer re-downloads everything — it recognises rides that already arrived via iCloud.

Small things

  • The AI Coach recovers automatically when Google retires the Gemini model behind your key (the "model no longer supported" error).
  • Settings > Invite a tester: QR codes and a ready-made invite card for both the Mac and iPhone/iPad versions.
  • Milestones on the Goals page: switch between Lifetime and This Year, the ladder re-centres on where you are, and passed milestones light up.
  • Plan sheets no longer clip their buttons on a short window, and sharing a plan PDF works properly.

ChainLink 0.3.492

Your full Garmin history, automatically.

Garmin Connect: the whole account, not the recent page

  • Connecting Garmin Connect now imports your entire ride history — it digs through the whole account in the background (200 rides at a time) and rides keep appearing as it works.
  • If you connected before and only got a handful of rides: open the app and press Sync once — the rest of your history follows on its own. Settings shows live progress while it runs.
  • Rides that fail to download (network hiccups, rate limits) are retried on the next sync instead of being skipped.

Small things

  • The sidebar's Coach entry now shows the full ⌘K shortcut.

ChainLink 0.3.489

Training plans get a bigger playbook — plus an invite kit.

Training plans now vary workout structure

  • Generated plans vary the structure of workouts, not just the length. A threshold day might be classic even reps one week, over-unders the next, then a climbing ladder; VO2 days rotate through 40/20 microbursts, hard-start reps and float sets; endurance days mix in sustained top-of-zone blocks and cadence drills.
  • No shape repeats until you have seen them all, and the session card always describes exactly what the exported workout will make you ride.
  • Existing plans keep their sessions — the variety appears in newly generated plans and rebuilt weeks.

Help us find more testers

  • Settings > Invite a tester now shows QR codes for both the iPhone/iPad and Mac versions, and can share or email a ready-made invite card with both links.

Milestones, plans and fixes

  • Milestones got interesting — switch between Lifetime and This Year on the Goals page; the ladder re-centers on where you are, passed milestones light up, and the next one shows live progress.
  • One active plan at a time — starting an event plan now ends a running maintenance block (with a note in its history) instead of counting the same rides toward two targets at once.
  • Maintenance plans gained the full action menu: adjust the block's hours and days, share it as a PDF, or end it.
  • Sharing a plan PDF now opens a working share panel (it could appear behind the window and be unclickable).
  • The plan-generation sheet could clip its buttons off-screen on smaller windows with no way to scroll — it now scrolls, and the buttons are always visible.
  • Leaving a ride's detail page via the sidebar could strand a blank page with a ghost back button — fixed.
